English: What are the best dairy cattle breeds for milk production in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī ng'ombe irīkū njega cia iria kūrīthia būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Friesian which has a large body frame (one of its main characteristics) and high milk yield.
Kikuyu: Friesian nī ng'ombe ya iria īrī mwīrī mūnene (īmwe ya ngumo nene yayo) na wūingī wa iria.

English: it has the ability to produce 20-25 Liters of milk per day with a butterfat content of 3.5%.
Kikuyu: īkoragwo na ūhoti wa kūruta lita 20 nginya 25 cia iria o mūthenya na 3.5% mūigana wa maguta ma butter fat.

English: Ayrshire which was developed in a region known for it's rolling hills.
Kikuyu: Ayrshire īgicoka guciarithanio gīcigo-inī kīrī na irīma nyingī.

English: The animal can survive in rugged terrain as the one it was carved out of.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe īno no īhote gūtūūra kūndū kūrī irīma ta kūria yaciarithanīirio.

English: Ayshire is characterized by the red and white colors that it carries on the skin.
Kikuyu: Aryshire īmenyekaga nīūndū wa matūūrī mayo mūtune na merū.

English: It has symmetrically shaped udders and a smooth body.
Kikuyu: Ningī nī īrī mūkamo wūigananīirie na mwīrī mūnyoroku.

English: Guernsey which originated from channel islands close to the north coast of France.
Kikuyu: Guernsey īrīa kīhumo kīayo nī Channel Islands hakuhī na rūgongo rwa rūteere rwa iria ria Faranja.

English: Their skins have shades of fawn with clearly defined white markings with a yellowish pigment.
Kikuyu: Nayo īno ikoragwo na matūūri merū na ngoikoni thitu

English: They carry horns that are inclined forward, refined with a medium length, and tapers towards the tips. The horns are also characterized by their small size and yellowish color at the base
Kikuyu: Guernsey ciarī na hīa nene ciarorete na mbere, ningī igakorwo na ūraihu wa gatagatī, igacekeha mithia-inī na igakorwo irī na rangi wa ngoikoni gītina-inī.

English: the Jersey breed which was developed in the largest Channel islands of Jersey.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba wa Jersey ūrīa waciarithanīrio Channel Island ya Jersey

English: What are the characteristics of a dairy cow?
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ya iria īkoragwo na maūthī marīkū?

English: Wide rump, pins and thighs enable a dairy cow to calve relatively easily.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ya iria īrī na ciīga nene cia thutha,iria imīteithagia ndīkagiīke mūno īgīciara.

English: A broad and big rib cage (barrel, stomach, chest) enables the animal to eat lots of feed for milk production.
Kikuyu: Makīria ma ūguo, gīthūri na nda nene nīihotithagia ng'ombe kūrīa irio nyingī, iria irī cia bata harī kūheana iria.

English: A steep foot angle is associated with better mobility, less hoof trimming, and longer life.
Kikuyu: Magūru marūngarū nī mahotithagia mīthīīre mīega, makanyihia gūconga kwa mahūngū na makaraihia ūtūro wa ng'ombe.

English: The hock joint is at a moderate angle, and the rear legs are straight.
Kikuyu: Maru ma magūrū ma thutha mabatiī kūrora ūndū ūkūhotithia magūrū mau kūrūgama marī marūngarū.

English: The hock is flat and clean and shows no swelling. Sickle legs put too much strain on leg muscles and tendons
Kikuyu: Ningī, mabatiī gūkorwo marī matheru na matarī maimbu. Magūrū magonyoku nīmagiragia ng'ombe īrūgame na njīra njega.

English: A large udder indicates high milk production. Ideally, quarters are evenly balanced, soft, and well-collapsed after milking.
Kikuyu: Mūkamo mūnene wonanagia iria rīingī. Ningī wīgayanitie maita mana maiganaine na ūhūaga wega thutha wa gūkamwo.

English: A high and firmly attached udder is essential for long-term dairy performance.
Kikuyu: Mūkamo mūnyitū na ūtari mūikūrūkū mūno ni wa bata hari kūheana ūmithio wa kahinda karaya.

English: High udders are related to less mastitis, fewer udder injuries, and longer life.
Kikuyu: Mūkamo wa mūthemba ūyū ndūrwaraga mūrimū wa mūkamo kana o na kūgia na nguraro kaingī.

English: Udders should not hang below the hock, but the cow's age needs to be considered.
Kikuyu: Mīkamo ndūagīrīire ngūcuha thī ma maru harī ng'ombe itarī ngūrū.

English: In addition, the udder should have little movement when the cow is walking.
Kikuyu: Makīria ma ūguo, ng'ombe igīthiī, mūkamo ndwagīrīirūo nī gūceera mūno.

English: Cows that are too thin or have a poor body condition at calving will produce less milk and are more likely to have fertility problems.
Kikuyu: Ningī ng'ombe īngīkorwo īrī hīnju mūno ihinda-inī ria gūciara, irūtaga iria inini na nīicokaga īkagīa na mathīna ma ūciari.

English: What are the factors that affecting successful breeding?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mangītūma gūciarithania ng'ombe kūgacīre?

English: Age at first calving: The animals selected must not be too mature or too young and selection for optimum body weight i.e., 55% of mature weight is necessary.
Kikuyu: Ūkūrū wa ng'ombe rīrīa iraciara riita rīa mbere: Ng'ombe īrīa īthuragwo ndīagīrīirwo gūkorūo īrī ngūrū mūno kana īrī nini mūno, na gūthurwo gwacio kūringana na ūritū ūrīa wagīrīire wa mwīrī, ūguo nī kuuga 55% ya ūrītū wa ng'ombe icio rīrīa ciīng'ima.

English: Therefore, when age at first service is 14-15 mts, the age at first calving becomes 22.5 to 23.5 months for maximum lifetime profit being.
Kikuyu: Nī ūndū ūcio, rīria ng'ombe yaheyo mūbira īna ūkūrū wa mieri ikūmi na īna nginya ikumi na ītano, ītanyagwo gūciara gītagatī-inī kīa ūkūrū wa mieri mīrongo īri na īri nginya mīrongo īri na īna nayo baida īkoneka kahinda-inī ka mūtūrīre wayo.

English: Calving interval: Rebreeding after calving depends upon the involution and uterine health of the cow.
Kikuyu: Ūtaganūkū wa maciara: Kūnyita nda īngī thutha wa gūciara, kuumanaga na kūhona na ūgima mwega wa nyūngū ya mwana.

English: Extension of inter-calving period from 12 to 14 months or more results into substantial reduction financial return over feeding costs as there will be loss in milk, calves produced per cow etc.
Kikuyu: Kūraihanīrīria mūno kwa maciara kuuma mīeri 12 nginya 14 nogūcūngirīrie ūnyihu wa baida ūkīringithania na irio iria ng'ombe īyo igūkorwo īrīte, ūnyihu wa iria na wa njaū iria īgūciarwo nī ng'ombe īyo.

English: Therefore, there will be considerable financial loss if the calving interval extends beyond 365 days in cows and 395 days in buffaloes.
Kikuyu: Nīūndū ūcio, nogūkorwo na unyihu wa baida angīkorwo maciara nīmekūraihanīrīria makīria ma mwaka ūmwe harī ng'ombe.

English: Inter service interval or interbreeding interval: It is the number of days between services.
Kikuyu: Interservice / interbreeding interval ni matuku maria ngombe yoyaga kuuma yaheo mbegu nginya rīrīa icokaga kuheo rīngī

English: Inter-service intervals of more than 40 days is common. To shorten it heat detection three weeks after insemination is useful.
Kikuyu: Nīguo kahinda gaka kanyihe, kūrora ndariri cia mūrukī kahinda-inī ka wiki ithatū thutha wa kūhe mbegū nīūndū ūrī bata.

English: Period of the standing heat: This depends on the efficiency of estrus detection in all cows.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīrīa ng'ombe īrūgamaga īna mūrukī nīguo īhaicwo: Nīkūringanaga na wathi wa kūmenya rīrīa ng'ombe īna ūrugarī.

English: But, cow fertility depends upon a number of factors, including the semen quality, correct handling of semen straws, AI-breeding technique and timing of insemination.
Kikuyu: Ūhoti wa ng'ombe kūgwatia ūringanaga na maūndū matiganīte ta wagīrīru wa mbegū, kūmenyerera mbegū na gūciga wega, kūhe mbegū na njīra njega na ihinda rīrīa riagīrīire.

English: Detection of estrus: For successful fertilization correct estrus detection is essential.
Kikuyu: Nīgetha ng'ombe īgwatie, he bata kūmenya ihinda rīrīa rīega rīa kūhe mbegū.

English: Approximately 50% of the estrous periods go undetected on the average dairy farm.
Kikuyu: Hakuhī nuthu ya ng'ombe cia iria nīciagaga kūmenyeka rīrīa irī na mūrukī.

English: Proper timing of insemination: Once Ovulation has occurred it is estimated that the viable life of the egg is less than 12 hrs, unless it becomes fertilized as Sperm require about 6 to 10 h to reach the lower portion of the oviduct.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīrīa rīega rīa kūhe mbegū: Rīrīa ng'ombe yagīa mūrukī, kwīrīkanaga atī itumbī rīikaraga muoyo mathaa manyihīire 12, tiga angīkorūo nīrīgūcemania na mbegū ya njamba tondū mbegū ciendaga mathaa matandatū nginya ikūmi nīguo ikinye mūkiha-inī wa gūikūrūkia itumbī.

English: Therefore the proper timing of insemination is to inseminate cows at 6 hrs after estrus (standing heat) so that adequate numbers of motile sperm are present in the oviduct.
Kikuyu: Gūkīri ūgūo, ihinda riega riakūhe ng'ombe mūbira nī mathaa matandatū kuuma yetīkira kūrūgama nīgūo īhaicūo.

English: Handling of semen straws while AI:To maintain maximum fertilization rates, shaking of the straw while thawing at at 37°C for at least 30 second.
Kikuyu: Ūmenyereri wa mībira ya AI; Nīguo kūgīe na ūma wa kugwatia, hebata mūbira ūgaikio maaī-inī mararū marī na ūrūgarī wa 37°C ūkīinainagio kahora kwa ihanda ria nuthu ndagīka.

English: Once thawed, inseminate immediately so as to prevent from cold or heat shock.
Kikuyu: Thutha ūyu, mbegū īno īkīrwo thīinī wa ng'ombe narūa nīgūo ndīgathūkio nī heho kana ūrugarī.

English: Embryonic losses: Early embryonic loss is critical during 15 or 16 days when the embryo is developed sufficiently to overcome uterine secretion of PGF2α.
Kikuyu: Kūhuna; Ūndū ūyū wonekaga thikū cia mbere 15 nginya 16 thutha wa ng'ombe kūoha nda.

English: Additional late embryonic losses can occur during the period of 25 to 40 days after insemination due to failure of attachment to the uterine wall.
Kikuyu: Ningī ūndū ūyū no wongerereke thikū-inī cia 25 nginya 40 thutha wa kūheo mbegū, nīūndū gacaū gatirī karenyitīrira wega thingo-inī cia nyūngū ya nyina.

English: Fertility and season : Hot season and high humidity decrease fertility of a cow due to reduction in appetite.
Kikuyu: Ūciari na ihinda ria kūhe mbegū; Ihinda rīa ūrugarī na ūgunyu mūingī riera-ini, nī itūmaga ng'ombe inyihie ūhoti wa kūgīa tūcaū tondū nīiremagwo ni kūrīa.

English: Heat stress reduces blood estradiol concentrations, progesterone levels, follicular activity and ovulation.
Kikuyu: Ūrugari mūingī nī ūnyihagia homoni cia uciari ona ningī gūkūra na kūingīha wa matumbī.

English: Provision of cooling systems may have a beneficial effect on fertility but the best time of fertility achieved in winter months.
Kikuyu: Kūgīa na indo cia kūnyihia ūrugari no kūrehe moimīrīra mega harī kūgīa njaū, no ihinda rīrīa rīega rīa kūgīa njaū rīkinyaga mīeri iria ya heho.

English: Dry period: Dry period is a critical component of a dairy cows.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīa gūtiga gūkamwo na kūruta iria; Rīrī nī ihinda rīabata harī ng'ombe.

English: Where duration of gestation cannot be shortened, but significantly affect the health or viability of the newborn calves.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ihinda rīa gūkua nda rītangīnyihanyihio, no rīhotomie ūgima wa mwīrī wa tūcaū tūrīa tūricoka gūciarwo.

English: Nutrients required during this period include the maintenance for the cow plus that required by the developing foetus.
Kikuyu: Irio iria ciendekaga ihinda-inī rīu rīa mieri ya muico ya nda nī hamwe irio iria ng'ombe īyo īkūīte nda ībataraga itukanīte na iria njaū īrīa īrathiī īgīkūraga ībataraga.

English: Usually 50 days is ideal for ensuing lactation.
Kikuyu: Kaingī ihinda rīa gūtiga gūkama kana kūruta iria rīagīriīre thikū 50.

English: How can I optimize reproduction efficiency in my dairy herd?
Kikuyu: Ingīka atia nīguo ngacīrithie gūciarana kwa ng'ombe rūru-inī rwakwa?

English: Optimizing reproductive efficiency depends upon the successful completion of the following events:
Kikuyu: Kūgacīrithia gūciarana kwa ng'ombe kūringana na maūndū maya:

English: (1) a heifer must reach puberty before the start of the breeding season
Kikuyu: (1) Mori no mūhaka yonanie mogarūrūku ma mwīrī mbere ya gwītia ndūme

English: (2) conceive early in the breeding season
Kikuyu: (2) Kūgīa ihu kīambīrīria-inī kīa ihinda ria gūcirithania

English: (3) calve unassisted
Kikuyu: (3) Gūciara ītegūteīthio

English: (4) the calf must survive to the time it is marketed
Kikuyu: (4) Njaū no muhàka īikare īgikūraga nginya rīrīa īkwendio

English: What are the available breeding methods?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra cia gūciarithia ng'ombe nī irīkū?

English: Natural mating, artificial insemination (AI), or embryo transfer
Kikuyu: Gūtwara ng'ombe kūri ndūme, kūhe mūbira wa AI, kana kūhe mūbira wa embryo

English: How do I synchronize estrus cycles for efficient breeding?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra irīku ingīrūmīrīra kūhotithia ng'ombe ciakwa gūītia ndūme irī hamwe?

English: This can be done by your local vet to ensure the usage of generally accepted and registered drugs/methods.
Kikuyu: ūhoro ūyū no wīkwo nī ndagītarī wa mahiū wa gīcigo kīanyu nīguo atigīrīre atī dawa iria īkūhūthīka nī iria jītīkīrie na ciendekithītio.

English: What are the nutritional requirements for lactating dairy cows to support successful breeding and calf health?
Kikuyu: Nī irio irīkū ciagīrīire kūheo ng'ombe cia iria nīgwo īhote kūnyita mūbira na njaū ikūre irī na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī?

English: Calcium and phosphorus supplementation amounts and ratio are important in young growing animals in support of bone development and in lactating cows for milk production
Kikuyu: Irio ciongereirwo calcium na phosphorus kwa mūigana ūria wagīriīre nī ciabata kūrī njaū irakūra gūteithīrīria gūkūria mahīndī na ng'ombe iria irakamwo nīūndū wa gūthondeka iria.

English: How can I monitor and manage reproductive health issues, such as infertility or low conception rates, in my dairy herd?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūrora na kūhiūrania na maūndū megiī ūgima mwega wa ūciari, ta kwaga kūgīa ciana kana kwaga kūgīa ihū, thīinī wa rūūru rwakwa rwa ng'ombe cia iria?

English: This can be achieved by managing breeding challenges by ensuring you use a breeding stock that has desirable genetic traits,
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū no ūhinge na njīra ya kūhiūrania na mathīna ma ūciari na gūtigīrīra atī ūracagūra ng'ombe cia gūciarithania iria irī na ngumo na maūthe mega ma ūciari,

English: good animal husbandry practices including supplying the required feeds in the right rations,control of pests and diseases.
Kikuyu: mītugo mīega ya ūreri wa ng'ombe, hamwe na gūcihe irio iria irabatarania na mūigana ūrīa wagīrīire, na kūrūa na tūtambi na mīrimū.

English: What are the benefits of using genetically superior sires or bulls for AI in dairy breeding programs?
Kikuyu: Nī ūguni ūrīkū umanaga na ūhūthīri wa iruka cia bata cia AI gūciarithania ng'ombe cia iria?

English: Use of superior sires ensures that you get calves that are superior in production,resistant to diseases, and good genetic characteristics/traits that are required to be passed to the offspring
Kikuyu: kūhūthīra iruka iria njega gūteithagia gūciarwo kwa njaū cia bata ikahota gūkorwo na umithio mūnene, ikahota gwītiria mīrimū, na maūthi ma bata marīa magathiī kwī njiarwa.

English: How do I develop a breeding plan or strategy tailored to my farm's goals and resources?
Kikuyu: Hihi mūbango mwega wa gūciarithania ng'ombe ūratwarana na muoroto wa mūgūnda wakwa na mahinya makwa ingīūthondeka atia?

English: A breeding plan varies from to farm depending on the availability of resources, the target market and the context challenges that a particular farm faces.
Kikuyu: Mūtaratara mwega wa gūciarithania ūringanaga na ūhoti na indo iria irī mūgūnda-inī, thoko na mathīna marīa makoragwo mīgūnda-inī ītiganīte.

English: What are the advantages of  Artificial Insemination (AI)?
Kikuyu: Hihi maūgūni ma gūciarithania na njīra ya kūhūthīra mūbira wa AI nī marīkū?

English: The ability to use sires of superior genetic merit (the best bulls of the breed).
Kikuyu: ūhoti wa kūhūthīra ndūme iria irī na ūhoti mūnene wa gūciara ng'ombe njega (Ndegwa iria njega mūno cia rūruka rūu).

English: In most cases producers have access to semen from the most genetically superior sires in a given breed at a cost far below what they would be required to pay if they were to purchase the natural service sire.
Kikuyu: Kaingī, arimi nīmahuthagīra mbegū kuuma kūrī ndegwa iria cionekaga irī njega mūno thīinī wa rūruka rūna, na thogora wacio nī mūnini gūkīra ūrīa mangīarīhire korwo nī ndūme ya kūhaica magūrire.

English: 2) Use of proven sires with high accuracy.
Kikuyu: 2). Ūhūthīri wa ndegwa iria irī wathi mūnene.

English: From a commercial standpoint, highly proven sires allows commercial cattlemen the opportunity to obtain genetics that is repeatable and provides a 2 high degree of accuracy for traits that they find desirable.
Kikuyu: Kūringana na ūndūire wa biacara, ndegwa iria njega nī itūmaga arīmi a ndūme cia biacara makorūo na mweke wa kūgetha mbegū nyingī irī na ūhoti mūnene wa gūciarithia ng'ombe njega na ikwendeka.

English: This is especially important when using AI in replacement heifers.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū nī wa bata makīria rīrīa mori cia gucenjia rūruka īkūheo mūbira wa AI.

English: Selecting semen from highly proven sires allows producers to identify matings that reduce calving difficulty.
Kikuyu: Gūthura mbegū cia ndegwa iria hītūkie nī gūteithagia arīmi kūmenya ng'ombe cia gūciarithania nacio nīgūo kūnyihia moritū maria monekanaga hindī ya gūciara.

English: 3) Producers have the ability to mate specific sites to individual cows.
Kikuyu: 3). Arīmi nīmari ūhoti wa kūmenya ndegwa iria mekūhūthīra kūhe ng'ombe mbegū.

English: This way producers may match desirable traits of cows and bulls for genetically superior calves.
Kikuyu: Ūū, arīmi no maciaranithie ng'ombe na ndegwa iria irī na maūthī na ngūmo iraringana nīgwo maciarīrwo njaū njega.

English: 4) AI provides an opportunity to reduce the number of herd bulls needed in cattle operation.
Kikuyu: 4). Kūhūthīra mbegū cia AI nī kūhotithagia arīmi kūnyihia ndegwa mīgūnda-inī yao.

English: One of the largest expenses that the average beef cattle operation incurs is the cost of maintaining a bull and repairing damage done by bulls to existing pastures and facilities.
Kikuyu: Ngarama īmwe nene mūno mūrimi wa ndegwa akoragwo nayo nīyagūcitungata na gūikara agīthondekaga kūrīa īrathūkia.

English: Reducing the need for herd sires reduces these maintenance costs.
Kikuyu: Kūnyihanyihia ndegwa nī kūnyihanyihagia ngarama ici.

English: 5) Improved genetics of offspring.
Kikuyu: 5). Njaū irī na maūthi na ngumo njega.

English: The offspring from cows that became pregnant to AI are usually genetically superior to natural service.
Kikuyu: Mori cia AI kaingī ikoragwo irī njega gūkīra iria cia kindūire kana iciarītwo nī ndegwa.

English: These offspring are later maintained in the herd as replacement females and will transmit improved genetics to their offspring.
Kikuyu: Thutha ūcio, mori icio nīicokaga ikarerwo nīgwo ikūrie rūūru rūu.

English: 6) Improved reproductive performance of the cow herd.
Kikuyu: 6). Ngo'mbe nyingī gūkorwo nīiraciara thīinī wa rūūru.

English: When used in conjunction with estrus synchronization the impact on reproductive performance of the cow herd is extensive.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa īhūthīrītwo hamwe na mūbango wa kūreka ng'ombe ciītie ndūme ihinda rimwe, rūūrū nī rūnenehaga naihenya.

English: Producers can have a reduced calving season, cows become pregnant earlier in the breeding season, and offspring are healthier and larger at weaning.
Kikuyu: Nao arīmi makagīa na ihinda ikuhī ria gūciarithia ng'ombe ciao, ng'ombe ikagīa mahu tene na njaū igakūra irī na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī na ūritū mūnene igītigithio kuonga.

English: What are the nutritional requirements for dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū maroragwo rīrīa tūkūhe ng'ombe cia iria irio?

English: A dairy cattle requires different nutritional requirements depending on age,size, lactating stage, environmental conditions among others.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ya iria nīrī mabataro ngūrani ma irio kūringana na ūkūrū, mūigana wa mwīrī, ihinda rīrīa īraruta iria, rīera na maūndū mangī maingī.

English: Generally the cow body needs energy,proteins,fibre,water and minerals/vitamins.
Kikuyu: Kwa njīra nguhī, mwīrī wa ng'ombe ya iria ūbataraga irio cia kūmihe hinya kana energy, proteins, fibre, maaī, cumbī o na vitamins.

English: How do I formulate a balanced diet for dairy cows?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa gūthondeka irio iria ciagīrīire ng'ombe ya iria?

English: For a healthy and productive cow, feed on a balanced diet.
Kikuyu: Nīgetha ng'ombe ya iria īkorwo na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī na īna ūhoti wa gūkūhe umithio, he bata kūmīhe irio iria ciagīrīire.

English: The ration should have the right amount and quality of concentrates, protein, mineral and vitamins.
Kikuyu: Igai rīa irio ici no mūhaka rīkorwo na gīthimo kīega kīa irio cia mūtu, proteins, maaī, bitameni na cumbī.

English: A dairy cattle ration should contain 70% energy source, 30% protein source and required minerals (such as: 35% wheat bran + 35% maize + 30% cotton seed cake + 1% minerals)
Kikuyu: Igai rīa irio cia ng'ombe ya iria ibatiī gūkorwo na gīcunje kia mirongo mūgwanja kia energy, mīrongo ītatū kia protein na mūtukanio wa macūmbī. Kwa mūhiano nī ta 35% wheat bran + 35% mbembe + 30% cotton seed + 1% cumbī.

English: What is the role of carbohydrates in the diet of dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Hihi bata wa irio cia hinya mīrīre-inī ya ng'ombe ya iria nī ūrīkū?

English: Carbohydrates are a source of energy for the cow.
Kikuyu: Carbohydrates nī irio iria iheaga ng'ombe hinya.

English: The cow needs energy for maintenance of major metabolic activities in the body including lactation,growth and development.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ibataraga hinya nīguo ciīga cia mwīrī wayo ihote kūruta wīra ta gūthondeka iria, gūkūra na gūthondeka mwīrī mwega .

English: The energy requirement varies from cow to cow depending on the size,environment,age
Kikuyu: Mūigana wa irio cia hinya ūringanaga na ūrīa ng'ombe īigana mwīrī, itūra na rīera na ūkūrū wa ng'ombe.

English: Why are proteins essential to dairy animals diet?
Kikuyu: Hihi protein nīyabata irio-inī cia ng'ombe ya iria?

English: Protein provides the essential chemical building blocks for the body cells and tissues, including muscles, blood, skin, internal organs, and also to make milk.
Kikuyu: Protein īheyanaga indo cia gūthondeka mwīrī hamwe na mīkiha, thakame, rūūa, cīīga cia thīinī cia mwīrī na gūthondeka iria.

English: Cows can make protein from non-protein nitrogen containing materials such as Urea and poultry litter.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe noīthondekere protein kuuma kūrī indo ta Urea kana mai ma ngūkū iria ikoragwo irī ihumo cia protein.

English: Cows cannot store much protein in their bodies and so it must be supplied in the daily ration in order to maintain high milk production.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ndīrī ūhoti wa kūiga protein nyingī mwīrī-inī na kwoguo he bata kūmīhe irio iria irī na protein nīguo kūmīhotithia gūthondeka iria rīingī.

English: If the amount of protein in ration is suddenly reduced, milk production will drop rapidly and the cow will lose weight, growth rate of calves and heifers will be reduced also.
Kikuyu: Protein īngīnyiha o rīmwe irio-inī, iria rikanyiha na ng'ombe īkahīnja, nacio njaū na mori igakūra kahora.

English: Feeding too much protein to dairy cows is wasteful because the excess is broken down by microorganisms in the rumen and excreted from the body.
Kikuyu: Nī wanangi kūhe ng'ombe protein nyingī makīria nīūndū īgayanagio na īkeherio mwīrī-inī kūgerera kīoro na mathugumo .

English: This is wasteful and is unlikely to happen since protein is very expensive.
Kikuyu: No kūrehe wūitangīku mūnene nīūndū proteini ī goro.

English: What are the sources of protein for dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Hihi kīhumo kīa irio mūthemba wa proteini harī ng'ombe cia iria ni kīrīkū?

English: soybean meal, canola meal, and alfalfa are the common sources of protein in dairy production.
Kikuyu: Mbegū cia soya, canola na alfafa nī imwe cia indo iria ikoragwo na protein īrī igūrū iria īhūthīkaga ūrīithi-inī wa ngo'mbe cia iria.

English: Protein is important for milk production hence an important requirement for lactating cows
Kikuyu: Protein nīyabata kūhe ng'ombe ya iria nīundū nī ībataranagia gūthondeka iria.

English: How do I calculate the energy content of dairy cow diets?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūmenya hinya ūrīa ūrī irio-inī cia ng'ombe ya iria?

English: What are the essential minerals needed for dairy cow nutrition?
Kikuyu: Nī cumbī ūrīkū wa bata irio-inī cia ng'ombe cia iria?

English: Calcium, phosphorus, potassium, magnesium,sodium chloride and trace minerals like copper, zinc, and selenium are essential for dairy production.
Kikuyu: Calcium, Phosphorus, Potassium, Magnesium, Sodium Chloride ona macumbī mangī mendagwo kwa ithimi nini ta copper, zinc na selenium nī mabata ūrīithi-inī wa ng'ombe ya iria.

English: Two macro-minerals are of particular importance. These are Calcium and Phosphorus and special attention should be given to the two minerals when formulating rations.
Kikuyu: Macumbī mūthemba wa calcium na phosphorus nī mabata mūno na no nginya tūkamaririkana rīrīa tūgūtūkania irio cia ng'ombe.

English: Legumes tend to have more Calcium and Phosphorus than grasses.
Kikuyu: Legumes īkoragwo na Calcium na Phosphorus nyingī gūkīra nyeki.

English: Grains are low in Calcium.
Kikuyu: Calcium īkoragwo ī nini thīīnī wa irio mūthema wa mbembe.

English: Young dark green forage  tends to have more minerals than old, dry and yellow forages.
Kikuyu: Mahuti meethī na maruru makoragwo na macumbī maingī kurī marīa makūrū, momū na marī na rangi wa ngoikoni.

English: Most tropical forages are low in Phosphorus.
Kikuyu: Mīmera mīingī ya kūrīa kūrī ūrugarī īkoragwo na ūnyihu wa phosphorus.

English: Sources of calcium include bone meal and dicalcium phosphate.
Kikuyu: Calcium yonekanaga kuuma mahīndī-inī na dicalcium phosphate, kana DCP.

English: Calcium and phosphorus requirements need to be balanced for the complete lactation cycle.
Kikuyu: Calcium na Phosphorus ibatiī kūheo ng'ombe ūtūūro-inī wayo woothe no no mūhaka ikorwo itūkanītio kwa ithimo iria ciagīrīire.

English: Minerals mobilized during early lactation must be replenished prior to the next calving to maintain cow health and performance.
Kikuyu: No mūhaka ng'ombe īcokie macumbī maria īkoragwo īhūthīrīte thutha wa gūciara mbere ya īciarite ringī nīūndū wa ūgima mwega wa mwīrī na kūruta iria wega.

English: It is  especially important to monitor the calcium and phosphorus levels during the late dry period (last four  weeks) to reduce the incidence of milk fever.
Kikuyu: Nī hari bata mūnene wa kūrora ithimo cia calcium na phosphorous makīria wiki-inī inya iria ng'ombe īkoragwo ītigītie īciare.

English: Calcium requirement during the dry period is .39 percentof ration dry matter, while the requirement for phosphorus is .24 percent.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīrī, ng'ombe īkoragwo īgībatara 0.39% ya calcium na 0.24% ya phosphorus thīinī wa dry matter.

English: Feeding calcium levels  above .39 percent may substantially increase the incidence of milk fever in your herd.
Kikuyu: Kūhe ng'ombe makīria ma 0.39% calcium no kūongerere mieke ya kūrwaria rūūrū rwakū milk fever.

English: Potassium. Legume forages are good potassium sources.
Kikuyu: Potasisium. Mahuti ma legumes nīmari wūingī wa potassium.

English: When corn silage is the major forage, or when  high levels of brewers grains are fed, potassium levels may be borderline deficient.
Kikuyu: Potassium nī īnyihaga rīrīa silage ya bebe kana ona macica ma iganda cia njohi megwīkirwo kwa wūingī irio-inī iria ng'ombe īrarīa.

English: When heat stress is a  problem, potassium requirements are increased to 3-5 percent of ration dry matter.
Kikuyu: Ningī, ūrūgarī ūngīingīha, nīhakoragwo hena bata wa kūongerera potassium na 3% nginya 5% irio-inī iria ng'ombe īkūria.

English: Magnesium requirement ranges from .10 percent for calves less than three months of age to .25- .30 percent for high producing cows.
Kikuyu: Magnesium nayo yendagwo kwa ithimo nini mūno cia kuuma 0.10% harī njaū cia ūkūrū ūtakīrite mieri ītatū nginya 0.25-0.30% hari ng'ombe iria iraruta iria rīinge.

English: Higher magnesium levels may be required during early lactation, when supplemental fat is fed, or when grass tetany conditions occur.
Kikuyu: Magnesium no ībataranie kwa wūingī hanini makīria mieri-inī ya mbere thutha wa ng'ombe gūciara, kana rīrīa supplemental fat cikūongererwo irio-ini iria ng'ombe īraria, kana rīrīa mūrimū wa grass tetany weyumīria.

English: Magnesium fed as magnesium oxide is often used as a buffer and results in ration magnesium concentrations of .30% or greater for alfalfa-based rations.
Kikuyu: Magnesium yongagīrirwo irio-inī ta magnesium oxide na gīthimo kia 0.30% kana ūkongerera rīrīa irio irī na mahuti ma lucern kana alfalfa.

English: Sodium chloride (common salt). The daily salt requirements for dairy cattle are met easily by adding 1 percent salt to a grain mix and offering additional salt free choice.
Kikuyu: Sodium chloride (cumbī ūrīa ūhūthagīrūo kaingī). Ng'ombe no ciiganie cumbī kwa njīra hūthū ya kūongerera 1% ya cumbī irio-inī cia mūtu hamwe na gūcigīra ūngī igacūna rīrīa ikwenda.

English: Lactating cows need 2 to 4 ounces of salt daily (about 1 ounce of salt per 30 pounds of milk production).
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe cia iria ibataraga cumbī mūnini mūno mūthemba wa common salt kana sodium chloride, ta gramu 28 harī o kilo ikūmi na inya cia iria rīrīa ng'ombe īraruta.

English: Dry cows need 5 ounces of salt daily. If udder edema is a problem, reduce salt intake during the last two weeks before calving.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe itarakamwo ibataraga grams igana ria mīrongo īna ya cumbi mūthemba wa sodium chloride.Mūrimū wa udder edema ūngīyumīria, hebata wa kūnyihanyihia common salt wiki igīrī cia mūico cia ng'ombe īrorete gūciara.

English: Copper. Most dairy rations need to be supplemented with copper either from trace mineralized salt or a premix containing a copper source such as copper sulf
Kikuyu: Copper. Irio nyingī cia ng'ombe nīibataraga kūongererwo cumbī wongereirwo copper.

English: How do I prevent mineral deficiencies in dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Ingīrigīrīria atīa ūnyihu wa cumbī kūrī mwiri wa ng'ombe cia iria?

English: Milking cows need minerals like MACLIK SUPER.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ya iria ībataraga cumbī kana indo cia kūiga mūthithū ta MACLIK SUPER.

English: Give about 1 glass per day.
Kikuyu: Gīthimo kīega nī ngirathi īmwe o mūthenya.

English: For dry and non-lactating cows, give FUGO DRY COW MINERAL fro Unga Farm Care.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ihingītwo gūkamwo, nīkuga irī ihinda kia mieri ya mūico ka ihu, kana noikorwo itirakamwo nīūndū wa gītūmi kīngī, noīheyo FUGO DRY COW kuuma kambuni ya Unga .

English: Granular mineral products should be mixed with feeds in a feeding trough while free lick should be allowed if it is in the form of a solid block
Kikuyu: Cumbī wa mūtu wagīrīire gūtukanio na irio naguo wa gūcūna wūigwo handū ng'ombe īrīcūnaga yenda.

English: What vitamins are important for dairy cow health?
Kikuyu: Nī vitamin irīkū cia bata harī ūgima mwega wa mwīrī wa ng'ombe ya iria ?

English: vitamins A, D, and E,These are group of substances that are required in the ration in very small amounts for  normal function of the body.
Kikuyu: Vitamin A, D na E nī ngurubu ya indo iria ibataranagia na gacunjī kanini mūno nīgūo mwīrī ūhote kūruta wīra wega.

English: Insufficient supply of any vitamin results in specific deficiency disease.
Kikuyu: Ūnyihu wa vitamin itiganīte kūigaga ng'ombe ūgwati-inī wa kūnyitwo nī mūrimū ūria wegemaine na ūnyihu wa vitamin īyo.

English: Vitamins are usually not added to dairy cattle rations because most of them are obtained from green forages and some are made in the rumen by microorganisms.
Kikuyu: Vitamin itiongagīrīrwo irio-inī nīūndū nyingī ya cio nīcionekaga mahuti-inī matarī momū na imwe igathondekerwo nda thīinī nī tūhengereta tūrīa tūteithagia gūthīa irio.

English: Why should I ensure my cows receive adequate water intake?
Kikuyu: Nī ūguni ūrīkū umanaga na gūtigīrīra ng'ombe nī yanyua maaī ma kūigana?

English: Animals need water for normal functioning of their body.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ibataraga maaī nīguo mwīrī wayo ūhote kūruta wīra wega.

English: Water is needed to make saliva for swallowing feed and for chewing the cud,
Kikuyu: Maaī nī mabata harī gūthondeka mata maria mahūthīkaga harī kūmeria irio na gūcokia ithagumia,

English: for feed to be digested, to cool the body when it is too hot
Kikuyu: Maaī no ma bata hari gūthīa irio, gūteithia kūiga mwīrī gīthimo-inī kīega kīa ūrugarī

English: and to remove waste materials from the body in the urine and faeces.
Kikuyu: Ningī nī mateithagia kūruta gīko mwīrī-inī kūgerera kīoro na mathugumo.

English: What role does fiber play in the diet of dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Itemi ria fibre nī rīrīkū irio-inī cia ng'ombe?

English: dietary fibre improves milk fat by increasing the amount of rumen acetate which acts as a precursor of milk fat synthesis.
Kikuyu: Fibre yongereraga maguta ma iria na njīra ya kuongerera mūigana wa rumen acetate īrīa īteithagīrīria gūthondeka maguta maya.

English: In fattening animals, dietary fibre helps control fat deposition in the carcasses and improves the marbling score sought by the consumer
Kikuyu: Ningī , fibre no īteithagia kūiganania maguta nyama-inī ūndū ūtūmaga nyama īyo ītwīke ya kwendeka nī mūgūri makīria nīūndū maguta nīmetukananagia wega na nyama.

English: What is the importance of roughage in dairy cow nutrition?
Kikuyu: Bata wa irio mūthemba wa mahut kūrī ng'ombe ya iria nī ūrīkū?

English: Roughage is fed to promote ruminal health and decrease digestive upset, roughage is a feed ingredient that has a high concentration of slowly degradable fiber.
Kikuyu: Irio mūthemba wa mahuti iteithagia kūgitīra mathīna ma nda ya ng'ombe na kūmīhe rīera rīega rīa gūthīa irio kahora.

English: How do I manage feed additives, such as buffers and yeast, in dairy cow diets?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūmenya gīthimo kīiganīru kīa indo iria ciongagīrīrwo irio-inī cia ng'ombe ya iria, iria nī hamwe na yeast na buffers?

English: Sodium bicarbonate, magnesium oxide or a combination are the primary buffers recommended for feeding lactating dairy cows
Kikuyu: Sodium bicarbonate, magnesium oxide kana mūtukanio wa indo ici nīwabata. Indo ici iria iteithagia kūrūgamia ūndū ūngīhītanire thīinī wa nda ya ng'ombe nīciongereragwo irio-inī cia ng'ombe.

English: What are the nutritional requirements for calves and heifers?
Kikuyu: Irio njega cia tūcaū na mori nī irīkū?

English: The three most essential nutrients for calf growth and development are water, energy and protein. Fibre, minerals and vitamins are also needed, but play a smaller role.
Kikuyu: Mithemba itatū ya bata ya irio cia gūtethia gacaū gūkūra na kūgimara nī maaī, irio cia kuhe mwīrī hinya, kana cia energy, protien, cumbī, na cia kūrūa na mīrimū kana vitamins. Vitamins cibataranagia kwa ithimi nini.

English: Water is essential for all living animals and it is good husbandry to provide calves with as much fresh, clean water as they want
Kikuyu: Maaī ni mabata hari nyamū irī muoyo na nī mītugo mīega kūhe tūcaū maaī maingī na matheru.

English: What are the potential risks associated with high-concentrate diets for dairy cows?
Kikuyu: Nī mogwati marīkū mangīrehwo nī kuhe ng'ombe cia iria irio cia mūtu kwa wūingī?

English: Cattle are fed a high-concentrate diet to improve their productivity; however, it alters the rumen ecosystem due to high structural carbohydrates level, resulting in ruminal acidosis
Kikuyu: Mahiū maheagwo irio cia mūtu kwa wūingī nīguo mongerere umithio no rīngī irio icio nī icenjagia mūrūtīre wa wira wa nda ya mbere, na rīngī mūrimū wa acidosis no wīyumīrie

English: How do I prevent acidosis in dairy cows?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūrigirīria ng'ombe cia iria kūnyitūo ni mūrimū wa acidosis?

English: Acidosis is a syndrome related to a fermentative disorder of the rumen resulting in overproduction of acid, which lowers rumen pH below pH 5.5.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa acidosis umanaga na irio kūgagata ūru thīinī wa nda ya ng'ombe ūgacungīrīria nda gūthondeka acidi kwa wūingī makīria iria ītūmaga pH īthiī thī wa githimo kia 5.5.

English: The problem is related to feeding management, where the ration has high levels of digestible carbohydrates and low effective fibre.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa acidosis ūrehagwo nī kwaga kūhe irio kwa gīthimo kīria kīagīrīire, igakorwo īrī na irio cia hinya itarī na fibre ya kūigana na nīūndū ūcio igathīīka narua.

English: Acidosis may be caused by diets high in readily fermentable carbohydrates and low in roughage, fast switch from high forage to high concentrate, excessive particle size reduction by feeding finely chopped forage.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa acidosis no ūrehwo nī kūhe irio nyingī cia mūtu gūkīra mahuti, gūcenjia mūrīre wa ng'ombe o rīmwe kuuma kūhe mahuti maingī nginya kūhe mūtu mūingī, ona kūhe mahuti matinangie tūcunjī tūnini makīria.

English: Signs of acidosis include Watery milk: Acetate, one of the end products of fibre digestion, is a precursor of milk fat synthesis.
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia acidosi nī hamwe na iria gūkorwo ta rīongereirwo maaī maingī, gītūmi nī tondū wa ūnyihu wa acetate īria yonekaga rīrīa fibre īrathīyo na igateitha gūthondeka maguta ma iria.

English: A diet that is low in fibre will lead to low levels of acetate in milk and thus low levels of milk fat. As a result, the milk appears watery.
Kikuyu: Irio itarī na fibre ya kūigana inyihagia acetate īrīa īthodekaga maguta ma iria. Ūndū ūtūmaga iria rīkahana ta riongereiro maaī.

English: Diarrhoea—Accumulation of acid causes an influx of water from the tissues into the gut resulting in diarrhoea.
Kikuyu: kūharwo - acidi īngīingīha thīinī wa nda nī ītūmaga maaī maume cīiga-inī ingī magoka mara-inī ūndū ūrīa ūtūmaga ng'ombe īkaharwo.

English: Faeces are foamy with gas bubbles and have mucin or fibrin casts.
Kikuyu: Kīoro gīgakorwo na mūhūyū na rūrenda.

English: Sore hooves (laminitis)—Endotoxins resulting from high acid production in the rumen also affect blood capillaries in the hoof, causing hooves to constrict, resulting in laminitis.
Kikuyu: Gūthua- acidi nyingī mwīrī-inī ningī nī ītūmaga ng'ombe īgathua nīūndū wa kūhotomia thakame iria īthiaga mahūngū-inī, mahūngū makaimba.

English: Ulcers, liver abscesses—High levels of acid in the rumen also cause ulcers, allowing bacteria to infiltrate the blood and causing liver abscesses, which are seen at post-mortem.
Kikuyu: Guthūka kwa ini - acidi nyingī thīinī wa nda nīirehaga tūronda twa nda. Nīūndū ūyū, njamuthi mūthemba wa bacteria nīihotaga kūingīra thakame-inī na igagūthūkia ini.

English: Prevention—Good management practices are needed to prevent predisposing situations from occurring. The root problem must be found and corrected.
Kikuyu: Kūrigīrīria - Mūtungatire mwega nī wabata nīguo kūrigīrīria maūndū marīa mangītūma ng'ombe īharwo. Kīhumo gia kūharwo no mūhaka kīmenyeke na gīthondekwo.

English: Buffers can also be used to prevent a drop in rumen pH when high-concentrate diets are fed.
Kikuyu: Buffers no īteithie kūrigīrīria kūgwa kwa ūrūrū wa nda kana githimo gia pH, angīkorwo irio nyingī cia mūtu nīcio ng'ombe īraheo.

English: Ensuring the presence of effective fibre in the diet promotes production of saliva, which is a buffer
Kikuyu: Tigīrīra irio irī na fibre njiganu nīgetha ng'ombe ihote kūruta mata maingī maria magiragia githimo kia pH gūikūrūka.

English: What feeding strategies can improve milk fat and protein content?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra irīkū cia kūhe ng'ombe irio ingīhotithia kūongerera maguta na proteini iria-inī?

English:
Feeding Strategies to Maximize Milk Solids include; proper ration formulation;
maximum feed intake; monitoring dietary composition (use routine forage, feed analyses);
Kikuyu: Njīra cia kūhe irio nīguo tuone iria imata nī hamwe na; gūthondeka irio njigananīru maūndū-inī moothe, kūhe ng'ombe irio īkahūna, gūthūrima irio kaingī kana nīirī na indo ciothe irabatarania na kaingī ūkahe ng'ombe mahuti.

English: harvesting and/or buying high-quality forage and proper forage allocation; properly feeding protein, energy, fiber, minerals and vitamins.
Kikuyu: kūgetha kana ona kūgura mahuti marī na ūkūrū ūria wagīrīire, kūhe proteini njiganu, irio cia hinya kana carbohydatres, fibre, cumbī na vitameni.

English: How do I adjust rations for high-producing dairy cows?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīka atia nīguo hote kūhūnia ng'ombe iria īrī na iria rīingī?

English: The ration should be available to the cows 22 to 24 hours a day.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe īratura iria rīingī no mūhaka ciigīrwo irio njega mathaa 22 nginya 24.

English: What is the importance of body condition scoring in dairy cow nutrition?
Kikuyu: Hihi gūthūrima ūrīa mwīrī wa ng'ombe ūhana nī gwabata ūrīkū mīrīre-inī ya irio?

English: Regular condition scoring allows farmers to monitor nutritional trends and provide sufficient warning to take action
Kikuyu: Gūthūrima mwīrī wa ngombe ya iria kaingī gūteithagia kūmenya kana irio iria īrarīa nīiramīagīrīra na ūndū ūyū ūkahe mūndū mwerekera wa ūrīa ūkūmīhe irio.

English: How do I manage nutrition for dairy cows in hot weather?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atia kūhe ng'ombe cia iria irio iria ciagīrīire rīrī kūrī na ūrugarī mūingī?

English: Feeding high quality fiber is preferred over minimal fiber diets during hot weather.
Kikuyu: Hebata kūhe irio īrī na fibre nyingī hīndī iria ūrūgarī ūrī mūingī.

English: What are the best practices for feeding dairy cows in cold weather?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra irīkū njega cia kūhe ng'ombe cia iria irio hīndī ya heho?

English: By using high-quality feed for dairy cattle is crucial during the cold season.
Kikuyu: Irio cia gīkīro kia igūrū na irī na hinya mūingī nī ciendekanaga hīndī ya heho.

English: The best feed for cattle in winter often contains grains like wheat, barley, rye, and maize on top of hay, forage, and silage.
Kikuyu: Irio njega nī iria irī na ngano, mwere, mūhīa na mbembe wongereīre nyeki nyūmithie, mahuti na silage.

English: How do I prevent metabolic disorders milk fever, through nutrition?
Kikuyu: īgīrigīrīria atīa mīrimū ya mīrīre ta ng'ombe kūremwo nī kūrūgama yaciara?

English: Milk fever is a common metabolic disorder in dairy cattle.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa ng'ombe kūremwo nī kūrūgama nī wonekaga kaingī kuri ng'ombe cia iria.

English: It generally affects older, high producing cows.
Kikuyu: Maita maingī, mūrimū ūyū ūrwaragwo nī ng'ombe ngūrū na iria iraruta iria rīingī.

English: Most milk fever cases occur within 48 to 72 hours of calving when demand for calcium for milk production exceeds the body’s ability to mobilize calcium reserves.
Kikuyu: Thīna ūyū wīyumagīria mathaa 48 nginya 72 thutha wa ng'ombe gūciara, rīrīa calcium īria yendekaga gūthodeka iria īredekana kwa wūingî gūkīra ūrīa mwīrī ungīhota kūmīthondeka.

English: Fever is amisnomer as body temperature is usually below normal.
Kikuyu: Ūrugarī wa mwīrī nī wūikūrūkaga thī ya haria wagīrīirwo.

English: Low blood calcium interferes with muscle function throughout the body causing general weakness, loss of appetite and eventually heart failure.
Kikuyu: Kūnyiha kwa calcium nī kūgiragia cīiga cia mwīrī irute wīra ūrīa cīagīrīire ūndū ūria ūcūngagīrīria kwaga hinya, kuremwo ni kūria na mūthia kūrugama kwa ngoro.

English: Signs include -the cow experiences muscle tremors, lack of appetite and unsteadiness.
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū ūyū nī kuinaina, kuremwo nī kūrīa na kwaga mītūkī.

English: Eventually, the cow is unable to rise, body temperature falls, and constipation occurs.
Kikuyu: Macūngīrīro nī ng'ombe kūremwo kūrūgama, ūrugarī wa mwīrī ūgaikūrūka thī ona kūūmīrwo nī kīoro

English: Cows go down to a sitting position often with a kink in the neck.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ikomaga ta ciikaire thī na igakūja ngingo.

English: Death may occur if the cow is not treated promptly.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe no īkūe īngīaga kūrigitwo na ihenya.

English: Managing the diet can be a valuable aid in preventing milk fever.
Kikuyu: Kūhe irio ūria kwagīrīire nī gwabata harī kūrigīrīria thīna ūyū.

English: The key to prevention is managing a dry (close-up) cow nearing parturition, which should be kept on a low calcium diet.
Kikuyu: Njīra njega ya kūrigīrīria thīna ūyū nī gūtiga gūkama ng'ombe iria īkuhīrīirie gūciara na njīra ya gūcihe irio ītari na cumbi mūingī wa calcium.

English: Such a diet stimulates the calcium regulatory system to keep the blood levels normal by mobilizing the body stores from the bone.
Kikuyu: Irio cia mūthemba ūyū nîihotithagia mwīrī kūmenyera kūhūthira calcium īrīa yaigīte mahīndī-inī ītamūthithū.

English: Lucerne, a feed high in calcium and potassium, should thus not be a major ingredient in close-up dry cow diets to avoid too high calcium levels before calving.
Kikuyu: Mahuti ma lucerne īrīa īkoragwo na calcium na phosphorus kwa wūingī, ndībatiī kūheo ng'ombe iria cikuhīrīirie gūciara kwa wūingī nīguo calcium ndīgakīre haria yagīrīire mbere ya gūciara.

English: When the demand for calcium increases at calving, calcium can be mobilized much more rapidly thus preventing milk fever.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa gūciara, calcium nīyendagwo kwa wūingī mwīrī-inī na nīūndū ūcio, ng'ombe nī īhotaga kūmīhūthīra o naihenya na kūrīgīrīria ng'ombe kūnyitwo nī thina wa milk fever.

English: In early lactation, high-yielding cows should receive as much calcium as possible.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe īrī na iria rīingī yagīrīire kūheyo calcium nyingī mīeri-inī ya mbere thutha wa gūciara.

English: High-risk cows can be injected with vitamin D3 2–8 days before calving
Kikuyu: Nacio iria īrī ūgwati-inī, ithecwo vitamin D3 thikū 2-8 mbere ya gūciara.

English: What are the nutritional requirements for dairy cows during the dry period?
Kikuyu: Nī mītheba īrīkū ya irio yagīrīire kūheo ng'ombe cia iria rīrīa īrī thīnī wa dry period, kana rīrī itarakamwo?

English: High yielding milking cows may drink 150 to 200 L water/d during the hot season.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe irī na iria rīingī no inyue lita 150 nginya 200 cia maaī o mūthenya rīrīa kwīna rīua rīhiū.

English: Ensure there is enough water
Kikuyu: Hebata wa gūciīgīra maaī ma kūigana.

English: How do I evaluate the quality of silage and other stored forages for dairy cow diets?
Kikuyu: īngīhota kūmenya atīa wagīrīru wa silage na mahuti marīa mangī ma ng'ombe?

English: Forage quality can be defined simply as the ability of the dairy cow to digest and utilize the nutrient components provided by the forage source
Kikuyu: Wagīrīru wa mahuti no ūtaūrwo kwa njīra hūthū irīa nīyo ūhoti wa ng'ombe kūhota gūthīa na kūhūthira nutrients kana irio iria irī mahuti-inī macio

English: What are the benefits of grazing for dairy cow nutrition?
Kikuyu: Nī moogūni marîkū mangiumana na kūrekereria ng'ombe cia iria kīeni-inī?

English: A cow that grazes has a choice on what to feed on,when and how to do as opposed to a confined cow.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ng'ombe īkūrīithio kīenī-inī, nīirī wīyathi wa gūthura kūrīa kīria īkwenda, rīrīa īkwenda ona njīra īrīa īkūrīa nayo. Ūū nī ūkīringithania na ng'ombe īhingīrītwo ciugū-inī.

English: This allows the cow to explore many eating options at hand.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū ūhotithagia ng'ombe ya kūrīithio gūkorwo na njīra nyingī cia gūthura kīria īkwenda kūria.

English: How do I optimize pasture utilization for dairy cow nutrition?
Kikuyu: Ingītungata atīa iharo cia kūrīithia ng'ombe nīguo cikorwo na umithio makīria harī kūhūnia ng'ombe?

English: When forages must be harvested, especially in times of scarcity,
Kikuyu: Rīrīa mahuti matwuīka no mūhaka matuo, makīria rīrīa kwīna wagi wamo,

English: there should be an emphasis on reaping as much fibre as possible with sufficient length to ensure the cow's gut functions properly and keeps her healthy.
Kikuyu: nī wega kūmagetha rīrīa marī makūrū wega, marī na fibre ya kūigana na ūraihu ūria wagīrīire nīguo nda ya ng'ombe īgaikara na rīera rīega rīrīa īkūria, na īgaikara īna ūgima mwega wa mwīrī.

English: How do I monitor and adjust feed intake to optimize milk production and cow health?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa gūthuthuria na kūgarūra mīrīre ya ng'ombe nīguo īhe iria rīingī na īikare īna ūgima mwega wa mwīrī?

English: Adjust the ratio accordingly to meet the changing needs of the cows and optimize performance.
Kikuyu: Hebata wa gūcenjia ithimo cia irio kūringana na mabataro ma ng'ombe.

English: Monitor Body Condition Score: Regularly assess the body condition score (BCS) of the cows
Kikuyu: Hebata wa gūikara ūgīthuthuragia mwīrī wa ng'ombe o ūrīa īrarīa.

English: What are the risks associated with mycotoxins in dairy cattle feed?
Kikuyu: Nī mogwati marīkū makuruhanīte na mīmera īrīa īkoragwo na thumu irio-inī cia ng'ombe?

English: Mycotoxins e.g aflatoxins  are naturally occurring compounds produced by fungi growing on plants in the field or during storage.
Kikuyu: Mīmera īrīa īkoragwo na thumu ta aflatoxin nī njamuthi cionekanaga kwa raithi mahuti-inī rīrīa mekūigwo kana o na marī mīgūnda-inī.

English: Feeding large quantities has resulted in decreased feed intake, decreased milk production, and some mild liver lesions.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ng'ombe ikūrīa njamuthi ici kwa wūingī, nīicokaga ikagiria ng'ombe kūrīa irio kwa wūingi, ikanyihia iria o na rīngī ini norīomboke hanini.

English: Consumption of feed contaminated with these toxins may cause mycotoxicoses in animals, characterized by a range of clinical signs depending on the toxin, and sometimes can lead to death of animals
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ng'ombe ikūrīa irio irī na njamuthi ici, nīcionanagia ndariri itiganīte kūringana na mūthemba wa njamuthi, na rīngī ng'ombe icio no ikue.

English: How do I manage fatty liver syndrome in cows?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atia kūhiūrania na mūrimū wa ng'ombe wa fatty liver?

English: Fatty liver disease is a disorder of highly productive dairy cows resulting from an excessive negative energy balance at the onset of lactation.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa fatty liver ūnyitaga ng'ombe iria īrī iria rīingī nīūndū wa gūkorwo īgībatara hinya mūingī wa gūthodeka iria thikū nini thutha wa gūciara.

English: Mobilization of large amounts of body fat reserves in response to insufficient dietary energy supply results in a transfer of fatty acids to the liver.
Kikuyu: Ūndū wonekanaga rīrīa ng'ombe yakorwo ndīraigania hinya kuuma harī irio iria īraheo na niūndū ūcio, yambagīrīria kūhūthīra mūthithū wa hinya, kana maguta, ūrīa yeigīire thīnī wa mwīrī wayo na rīngī maguta maya nīmakīraga makaīngīra ini-inī.

English: Excessive amounts are deposited in the hepatocyte as trigylcerides and can result in disturbed liver function and liver cell injury.
Kikuyu: Maguta mangīkīra thīinī wa ini, nīmarīthūkagia.

English: The condition is associated with pronounced ketosis, feed intake depression, and decreased productivity; severe cases lead to liver failure and a fatal outcome.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū wītagwo ketosis ūrīa ndariri ikoragwo nī hamwe na ng'ombe kūremwo nī kūrīa, ūnyihu wa iria, na ketosis īngīneneha, ini rīthukaga biū na ng'ombe īgakua.

English: Diagnosis can be made directly by determining the liver fat content but is most commonly done indirectly by assessing severity and duration of negative energy balance.
Kikuyu: Kūmenya kana ng'ombe nīnyite nī mūrimū ūyū, njīra ya gūthuthuria mūigana wa maguta thīinī wa mwīrī no īhūthīke, no kaingī kūhūthagīrūo njīra ītarī ya īmwe kwa īmwe nayo nī kūrora ihinda rīrīa ndariri ikoretwo ikīoneka.

English: How do I ensure my cows receive adequate nutrition while grazing on pasture?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota kūgacirithia atia kūhūna kwa ng'ombe iria irīithagio cieni-inī?

English: If grazing, consuming hay, or feeding on other harvested roughage, cattle should either be provided free-choice access to a complete mineral supplement.
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo ng'ombe īrarīithio weru-inī, kana iraheo nyeki nyumithie, nīciagīrīirwo kūheo cumbī mūiganu ūtari wa gūthimīrwo.

English: This complements the forage base of the ration or be given another supplement that contains the amount necessary to meet the animal's requirements.
Kikuyu: Cumbi mūiganu na indo ingī iria citeithagia kūhingia mabataro ma mwīrī wa ng'ombe .

English: What are the nutritional needs of dairy cows during the transition period between dry and lactating stages?
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ībataraga irio cia mūthemba ūrikū rīrīa irī gatagati-inī ga gutigithio gukamwo na hīndī īria irakamwo?

English: To prevent impaired immune function, add appropriate amounts of trace minerals and vitamins
Kikuyu: Nīguo kūgiria kūgūmīrwo nī mīrimū, hebata kūhe ng'ombe ithimi iria ciagīrīire cia cumbī na vitamins.

English: How do I assess the body condition of my dairy cows and adjust their diet accordingly?
Kikuyu: Hihi īngīhota atia gūthuthuria mwīrī wa ng'ombe ciakwa nīguo hote gūcicenjeria irio na njīra iria yagīrīire?

English: This can be done by regular scoring of the cow to ensure you meet the health and nutrition of the cow at a particular stage.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū ūngīkīka na njīra ya gūthuthuria kaingī ūrīa mwīrī wa ng'ombe ūhana nīguo ūgima wa mwīrī wayo ūgaikaraga ūrī mwega hamwe na mīrīre yayo.

English: This will also avoid overfeeding/underfeeding of the cow and detection of health challenges on time.
Kikuyu: Gūthuthuria gūkū gūteithaiga wanangi wa irio kana kūhe ng'ombe irio nini o na nīngī nīgūgateithia kūmenya rīrīa mūrimū ūracema ng'ombe.

English: What are the nutritional requirements replacement heifers?
Kikuyu: Mori iria ireragwo igatuīka rūūru rwa rūciū ibataraga irio irīkū?

English: heifers that are meant to replace/replacements should be managed so that they reach 60-65% of their expected mature body weight prior to the breeding season.
Kikuyu: Mori irongoreirio gūtwīka rūūru rwa rūciū ibatiī gūkorwo ikinyītie ūritū wa 60-65% ya kilo yacio rīrīa irī ng'ima ihinda ria kūheo ndume rīa mbere.

English: This way they ensure a strong flock ready to produce
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū ūhotithagia kūgia na rūūrū rwīna ūhoti wagūciarana.

English: What are the potential consequences of inadequate mineral nutrition in dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Nī maūmīrīra marīkū marehagwo nī wagi kana ūnyihu wa cumbī irio-inī cia ng'ombe cia iria?

English: Even small imbalances or deficiencies can develop into reproductive, health and milk production problems.
Kikuyu: Wagi wa cumbī irio-inī cia ng'ombe kana o na kwaga wūigananīru mūnini wa cumbī, kūhotomagia ūgima mwega wa mwīrī, maciaro na kūrehe ūnyihu wa iria.

English: As herd milk production increases, it will become more critical to balance and fine-tune the dairy cow’s mineral and vitamin feeding program.
Kikuyu: O ūrīa iria rīraingīha thīinī wa rūūru, hebata gūthondeka mūtaratara mwega wa kūhe cumbī na vitamins.

English: Generally, the two sources of minerals include natural (organic) feeds (forages and grains) and inorganic mineral supplements to balance the minerals present in the forages and grains.
Kikuyu: Kaingī, macumbī moimaga irio-inī iria ng'ombe īrarīa ta mahuti na mbembe, kana marīa magūthondekwo, marīa makoragwo marī makuongererwo nīguo īiganie cumbī ūrīa īraruta mahuti-inī na irio-inī ta mbembe.

English: How do I prevent nutritional imbalances in my dairy cow herd?
Kikuyu: īngīgitīra atīa wagi kana ūnyihu na kwaga kūiganana kwa irio harī ng'ombe cīa gūkama?

English: This can be achieved by ensuring the dairy cattle are fed a ratio that is  balanced for protein, energy, fiber, vitamins and minerals
Kikuyu: Nī wega kūhe irio ciigananīirie indo ciothe iria ciendekaga thīnī wa mwīrī iria nī hamwe na protein, energy ,vitamins, fiber na cumbī.

English: What are the nutritional requirements for dairy cows producing colostrum and milk for newborn calves?
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe njiairī īraruta gīthana ībatiī kūheo irio ihana atia?

English: The three most essential nutrients for calf growth and development are water, energy and protein.
Kikuyu: Nī ūndū wa gūkūra kwa mwīrī wa njaū na njīra njega, maaī, energy na protein nīcio cia bata makīria.

English: Calves also requires Fibre, minerals and vitamins , but play a smaller role
Kikuyu: Njaū nī ībataraga fiber, cumbī na vitamins no kwa ithimo nini.

English: How do I calculate the cost-effectiveness of different feed options for my dairy cows?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra īrīkū njega ya kūringithania thogora wa irio itiganīte īngīhee ng'ombe ciakwa?

English: feed costs and farm gate price of milk determine the profitability of a dairy enterprise.
Kikuyu: Ngarama ya irio na thogora wa iria nīcio ituaga baida ya ūrithi.

English: An enterprise may opt to produce the maximum amount of milk irrespective of the profit margin.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda mūna no ūkorwo wīna mūoroto wa gūkama iria rīingī biū hatarī gwīkīrīra baida īria īronekena.

English: This is termed maximum production.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū wītagwo mūigana ūria mūnene wa magetha.

English: If the milk price is high, this method may coincide with maximum profit.
Kikuyu: Thogora wa iria ūngīkorwo wīmūnene, nogūo baida īnenehaga.

English: On the other hand, an enterprise using home-grown forage may or may not produce the maximum amount of milk but makes a profit.
Kikuyu: Tūkīrora mwena ūngī, mūgūnda ūhūthagīra mahuti magwīkūrīria, wahota kwaga gūkama iria rīingī no noūthondekaga baida.

English: This happens when the cost of concentrate is too high.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū wonekaga rīrīa irio cia mūtu irī nagoro mūno.

English: Farm gate price of milk also depends on location, with peri-urban farmers getting better prices.
Kikuyu: Thogora wa iria ningī nīūringanaga na kūria mūgūnda ūri, kana itūra.

English: A true maximum profit ration program includes a least-cost function, incorporates milk price information, and uses maximum profit (income over feed cost) as one of the constraints or specifications on which to formulate.
Kikuyu: Mīgūnda iria īkūhīrīirie mataūni nīyendagia iria na thogora mwegega. Mūtaratara mwega wa gūthondeka baida njega wonekaga rīria ngarama yakūrīithia ī nini, na thogora wa iria nī ūīkaine.

English: What are the nutritional needs of dairy cows during the breeding season?
Kikuyu: Nī irio cia mūthemba ūrīkū ciagīrīire kūheo ng'ombe cia iria hindī ya gūciciarithania?

English: Heifers can be reared on good-quality pasture as their nutrient requirements are low (growth and maintenance).
Kikuyu: Mori no cirīithīrio ciieni-inīnjega nīgukorwo ūbatari wa irio kana nutrients ti mūnene (gūkūra na kūgacīrithia).

English: Supplementation with concentrate should be at 1% of body weight with 12–14% crude protein for heifers on legume forage and 15–16% crude protein on grass forage.
Kikuyu: Irio cia mūtu ciagīrīire kūongererwo na gīthimi kīa 1% kīa ūritū wa mori na 12-14% proteini iria cīigīirwo mahuti mūthemba wa legumes na 15-16% angīkorwo nī nyeki.

English: Protein is extremely important in the diet of growing heifers to ensure adequate frame size, wither height and growth.
Kikuyu: Protein nī yabata mūno kūrī mori irakūra nīguo kūgacīrithia ūnene, ūraihu ona kūongerera ūritū.

English: Avoid short heifers by balancing the ration and including enough crude protein.
Kikuyu: Wītheme mori nguhī na njīra ya gūcihe irio njega īrī na protein ya kūigana.

English: Feeding heifers too much energy leads to fat infiltrating the mammary glands, inhibiting development of secretory tissue, thus reducing milk yield.
Kikuyu: Mori īngīheo irio nyingī cia hinya, mūkamo ndūkuraga wega niūndū wa maguta kūingīra nyondo-inī na no yage kūruta iria wega ihinda rīakinya rīa gūkamwo.

English: Underfeeding results in small-bodied heifers, which experience difficulty during  calving (dystocia).
Kikuyu: Ningī kūhe irio nini gūcungagīrīria mori ikagīa mīrī mīnini, ūndū ūyū ūkarehe ūgwati hindī ya gūciara yakinya.

English: Overfeeding heifers on feed high in energy but low in protein results in short, fat  heifers; high protein and low energy feed results in tall, thin heifers
Kikuyu: Kūhe mori irio nyingī cia hinya na protein nini gūtūmaga ikūre irī noru na igakuhīha, ningī ungīcihe irio cia hinya itarī njiganu īgatūma mori ikūre irī njeke na ndaihū.

English: How do I handle problems associated with high temperatures that lead to herd stress?
Kikuyu: īngīhota atia kūhiūrania na mathīna marīa marehagwo nī ūrugarī mūingī ūgatuma ng'ombe īgiīke?

English: By providing enough water. You may also ensure ventilation within the dairy unit by providing enough ventilations or introducing fans
Kikuyu: Thīna ūyū ūrūagwo naguo na njīra ya kūhe maaī maingī. Ningī nowikīre huhirio kana wikīre feni cia kūhurutīra thīinī wa ciūngū cia ng'ombe.

English: What are the nutritional requirements for dairy cows in different lactation stages?
Kikuyu: Nīmūrīre ūrīku wagīrīire kūheo ng'ombe cia iria mahinda ngūrani ma mūtūrīre wayo?

English: Carbohydrates, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins, and water are all important at this stage.
Kikuyu: Carbohydrates, amino acids, fatty acids, cumbī, vitamins na maaī ciothe nī ciabata mahinda mothe.

English: Good animal husbandry starts at breed selection and good animal husbandry practices.
Kikuyu: Ūrīithi mwega wa mahiū wambagīrīria rīrīa ng'ombe cia gūciarithania iracagūrwo na kūrūmīrīra mītugo mīega ya ūrīithi.

English: How do I manage nutrition for dairy cows in intensive housing systems?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atia kūgacīrithia mīrīre ya ng'ombe iria ihingagīrwo?

English: This can be done by managing your herds in groups.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū ūngīhotekeka na njīra ya kūgayania ng'ombe na ikundi cia rika.

English: Ensuring that you group your cows into age groups so that you provide feeds depending on the needs of each cow.
Kikuyu: Ūgūcihe irio kūringana na bata wa ngurubu īyo.

English: How do I optimize feed efficiency and reduce feed waste in dairy cow nutrition programs?
Kikuyu: Ingīgacīrithia atia mīhere na kūnyihia wanangi wa irio ūrīithi-inī wa ng'ombe ya iria?

English: Regularly evaluate the nutrient composition of feed ingredients to ensure accurate ration formulation.
Kikuyu: Hebata gūthūrima mūigana wa nutrients irio-inī nīguo gutigīrīra irio icio nī nginyaniru.

English: Conduct feed tests to determine the quality and nutritional value of the feed.
Kikuyu: Nīwega gūthima irio cia ng'ombe nīguo kūmenya kana nīirī na ūkinyanīru wa nutrients ūrīa wagīrīire.

English: Optimize Forage Quality: Forages, such as hay and silage, are crucial components of a dairy cow's diet.
Kikuyu: Mahuti ta silage o na marīa momithie ta nyeki, nī mabata ūrīithi-inī.

English: Keep track of the amount of feed consumed by each cow.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūmenya githimi kia mūigana wa irio iria ng'ombe irarīa.

English: This information helps identify any variations or potential issues and allows adjustments to be made to the ration if needed.
Kikuyu: Ūhoro ūyū ūteithagia kūmenya ūtiganu maūndū morū marīa mangįkorwo makīyumīria kana ona ūtiganu ūria ūngikorwo wīho na kūhotithia kwoya ikinya rīrīa riagīrīire.

English: Incorporating by-products and alternative feeds can be a cost-effective strategy.
Kikuyu: Gūtukania matigari na mīthemba īngī ya magetha no ūkorwo ūrī mūbango ūtarī na goro.

English: For example, distillers’ grains, bakery waste, or citrus pulp can be used as alternative feed sources.
Kikuyu: Kwa mūhiano, matigari ma īganda cia njohi, mīgate o na macica nī imwe cia ihumo cia irio cia ng'ombe.

English: However, consult with a nutritionist to ensure proper inclusion levels and maintain a balanced diet.
Kikuyu: O na kūrī ūguo, hebata gwetha mūtaramu wa mīrīre mīega nīguo agūteithie gūthondeka irio njega cīgananīrie maūndū mothe.

English: What are the common diseases affecting dairy cows in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mīrimū īrikū īgūmagīra ng'ombe cia iria mūno gūkū Kenya?

English: mastitis,anthrax,Black Quarter,Foot-and-mouth cattle disease,Milk fever cattle disease
Kikuyu: Mīrīmū ino nī hamwe na mastitis, mūūrirū, foot and mouth na milk fever.

English: How do I prevent and control mastitis in my dairy herd?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atia kūgitīra na kūrūa na mūrimū wa mūkamo rūūru-inī rwa ng'ombe cia iria?

English: In Kenya, Mastitis, or inflammation of the mammary gland, is the most common and the most expensive disease of dairy cattle.
Kikuyu: Gūkū Kenya, mūrimū wa mūkamo, kana kūimba kwa mūkamo, nī ūkoragwo ūkīoneka kaingī mūno na ūbataraga mbeca nyingī kūrigita.

English: Although stress and physical injuries may cause inflammation of the gland, infection by invading bacteria or other microorganisms (fungi, yeasts and possibly viruses) is the primary cause of mastitis.
Kikuyu: O na gūtuīka gūtangīka na hihi ngūtihio no gūcūngagīrīria ng'ombe kūrwara mūrimū ūyū, bacteria na njamuthi ingī ta fungi,yeast na virus, nīcio irwaragia ng'ombe mūrimū wa mūkamo.

English: Infections begin when microorganisms penetrate the teat canal and multiply in the mammary gland.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū wambagia kuoneka rīrīa njamuthi ici ciingīra nyondo-inī na gūciaranīra kūu thīinī.

English: What are the signs and symptoms of mastitis, and how do I detect it early?
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa mūkamo wonanagia ndariri irīkū na ingīūmenya atīa mbere ya ūtheremete?

English: Symptoms include swelling, pain, and redness of the udder.
Kikuyu: Ndariri nī hamwe na kūimba, ruo na gūtunīha kwa mūkamo

English: How do I implement a mastitis prevention program on my farm?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa gūthondeka mūtaratara ngūrūmīrīra wa kūrigīrīria mūrimū wa mūkam mūgūnda-inī wakwa?

English: By using proper milking technique in the parlor to prevent teat injury and cow-to-cow transmission of pathogens.
Kikuyu: Nīwega gūkamīra ng'ombe thīinī wa mūthaigū nīguo kūrigīrīria nyondo kūgīa na nguraro o na gūtambia njamuthi kuuma kūrī ng’ombe imwe nginya īrīa īngī.

English: Routine use of germicidal teat dip applied after milking.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūrūmīrīra mūtugo wa kūrikia nyondo dawa-inī ya kūūraga njamuthi thutha wa gūkama.

English: Antibiotic dry cow therapy used according to label specifications
Kikuyu: Ningī nī wega kūrūmīrīra mītaratara ya ūhūthīri wa dawa īrīa yagīrīire kūringana na mataaro ma mūthondeki.

English: Is east coast fever vaccinations necessary to protect dairy cows and how often should they be administered?
Kikuyu: Njanjo cia mūrimū wa ngaaī nīigitagīra ng’ombe cia iria na hihi ng'ombe ībatiī gūthecwo maita maigana?

English: ECF Muguga Vaccine is a quality vaccine and is used successfully across East, Central and Southern Africa (Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Malawi, Zambia and Zimbabwe) against east coast fever.
Kikuyu: Njanjo ya mūrimū wa ngaaī īrīa ītagwo ECF Muguga Vaccine, nīya gīkīro kīa igūru na nī īhūthīkīte na īkonania mawega mabūrūri marīa marī rūgūrū, gatagatī na mūhuro wa Africa (Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Malawi, Zambia na Zimbambwe) harī kūhūrana na mūrimū wa ngaaī.

English: It is still necessary to spray against ticks as ticks carry other disease besides mild exposure to ticks is important to keep your animals immune for life.
Kikuyu: Hebata wa kūhūra ng'ombe dawa ya kūūraga ngūha tondū nīitambagia mūrimū wa ngaaī na mīrimū īngī. Ng'ombe ibatiī kūigwo na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī hīngo ciothe.

English: Ask your vet for advice on how often to spray against ticks.
Kikuyu: ūria ndagītarī wa mahiū maakū rīrīa ūbatiī kūhuhīrīria dawa ya ngūha.

English: Can be used in calves from one month of age.
Kikuyu: Njanjo ya ngaaī no īheo njaū ya ūkūrū wa mūeri ūmwe.

English: One vaccination shot protects your animal against ECF for life.
Kikuyu: Njanjo īmwe tu īgitagīra ng'ombe kuumana na mūrimū wa ngaaī mūtūrīre wayo woothe.

English: How do I prevent and control tick infestations in my dairy herd?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūrigīrīria na kūrūa na ngūha rūūru-inī rwakwa ?

English: Rotational grazing management in conjunction with planned chemical treatment is an effective means of cattle tick control.
Kikuyu: Ūmenyo wa kūrīithia na njīra ya gūthamia ng'ombe kuuma kieni-inī kimwe nginya kīrīa kīngī hamwe na kūrūmīrīra mūtaratara wa ūhūthīri mwega wa dawa, nī njīra īmwe īngīrehe umithio mwega wa kūrua na ngūha.

English: Livestock owners reduce the effects of ticks mainly through tick control using chemicals known as acaricides.
Kikuyu: Arīithi a mahiū mahūthagīra dawa cia mūnanda kūūraga ngūha.

English: Acaricides come from different groups identified by their active ingredients, with different modes of action to kill the ticks.
Kikuyu: Mīhīrīga nī mīingī ya dawa cia mūnanda kūringana na indo iria icithodekete na mūrutire wacio wa wīra.

English: Amitraz, synthetic pyrethroids, organophosphates or combinations of these active ingredients are some of the commonly available acaricide in the market.
Kikuyu: Amitraz, pyrethroids, organophosphates kana mūtukanio wa ici ciothe nī imwe cia dawa cia mūnanda iria irīthoko-inī.

English: What are the best practices for deworming dairy cows?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra irīkū njega cia kūrūa na mīnyoo kana njoka ng’ombe-inī cia iria?

English: It is recommended to deworm once in three months but this can be shortened when the worm burden is high.
Kikuyu: Nīkwedekithitio kūhe ng’ombe dawa cia njoka rita rīmwe harī mīeri ītatū no ihinda rīrī no rīnyihe angīkorwo mīnyoo nī mīingī mūno.

English: Wet and humid conditions for animals in pasture is a period of greatest worm challenge and strategic deworming should come at such times.
Kikuyu: Ūgūnyu mūīngī cieni-inī nīnī ūtūmaga njoka ikaingīha mūno na nīūndū ūcio, nīwega gūthondeka mūtaratara mwega wa kūrūa nacio.

English: To control worms, deworm your herd.
Kikuyu: Nīguo kūrūa na mīnyoo, rūūru rwothe rūheo dawa.

English: It is recommended to do drenching when deworming to ensure the cow swallow the dose of the drug.
Kikuyu: Nī kwendekithītio atī dawa ya njoka īheo na njīra ya gūkundio nīguo imerie yothe.

English: Dose calves with recommended anthelmintics at 4 months and every 6 months thereafter.
Kikuyu: Njaū nī ibataīre kūheo dawa cia mīnyoo īrī na mīeri īna na ikarūmīrīra kūheo o thutha wa mīeri ītandatū.

English: Is anthrax vaccinations necessary to protect dairy cows and how often should they be administered?
Kikuyu: Hihi njanjo cia mūrimū wa mūūrirū nī cia bata harī kūgitīra ng'ombe na ibatiī kūheo maita maigana?

English: In Kenya, Anthrax is the highest ranked zoonotic disease, based on a systematic analysis of burden, socio economic impact, epidemic potential, and severity of zoonotic diseases.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa mūūrirū būrūri-inī wa Kenya nīguo ūtongoretie harī mīrimū īria īgwatanagio kuuma kūrī nyamū gūthiī kūrī andū. Ūū nī kūringana na ūthuthuria ūrīa wonanītie atī mūrimū ūyū nīūgūmagīra andū na biacara ciao na nīngī no ūthereme kūnene gwa kahinda kanini.

English: According to national public health and veterinary records, an average of 10 Anthrax outbreaks involving animals with spill- over to humans occur every year.
Kikuyu: Riboti ya national public health and veterinary yonanītie atī mītuthūko o ikūmi ya mūrimū wa mūūrirū nīīgwatagio andū o mwaka.

English: Anthrax is also a priority disease in Kenya’s National Action Plan for Health Security (NAPHS).
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa mūūrirū ningī nī wīkīragīrwo mūno mūtaratara-inī wa National Action Plan for Health Security (NAPHS), Kenya.

English: Annual vaccination for three consecutive years after an outbreak to break the infection cycle in an area is recommended. Always consult your local vet for recommendations.
Kikuyu: Nīkwedekithītio atī njanjo irūmanīrīrio mīaka ītatū thutha wa mūtuthūko itūrū-inī rīna nīguo kūnina mūrimū wa mūūrirū kaimana. Nīwega hīndī ciothe kwaranīria na ndagītarī wa mahiū nīguo agūtare.

English: How do I manage foot and mouth disease in dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūrūa na mūrimū wa foot and mouth. (mūrimū wa magūrū na kanua)

English: How do I manage and prevent internal worms in dairy cattle?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī njīra īrīkū ingīhota kūrigīrīria ndangūrū na mīnyoo ng'ombe-ini cia iria?

English: This can be done by maintaining a clean environment and ensuring a proper feeding program.
Kikuyu: Mīnyoo īgirīrīkaga na njīra gūthingata ūtheru mūnene na kūrūmīrīra mūtaratara mūkinyanīrū wa kūhe ng'ombe irio.

English: Always consult your vet to  assist in coming up with a deworming program together and recommendations.
Kikuyu: Aranīria na ndagitarī wa mahiū ūgīthondeka mītaratara ya kūhe ng'ombe dawa cia mīnyoo.

English: What are the signs of reproductive disorders in dairy cows, and how do I address them?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia mathīna ma ūciari ng'ombe-inī cia iria nī irīkū na ingīrigīrīria mathīna maya atīa?

English: The efficiency of reproduction is influenced by the interactive effect of environment, management, and genetic factors.
Kikuyu: Ūhoti wa ūciari ūcangagīrwo nī maūndū marīa matūrigicīirie,mītungatīre na maūthī ma ūciarī.

English: Furthermore, diseases commonly affect dairy cow productivity by decreasing reproductive efficiency, shortening the expected length of productive life, and lowering milk production.
Kikuyu: Ningī mīrimū ya ng'ombe cia iria nī inyihagia maciaro kaingī na njīra ya kūnyihia matukū marīa ng'ombe īikaraga īngīciaraga na īkanyia iria.

English: The major reproductive problems are anestrus, RB, retention of fetal membrane, and abortions which contributed >80% of the total affected animals.
Kikuyu: Mīrimū ya ūciari nī hamwe na kwaga kūonania mūrukī, RB, kūremwo nīkūruta thigira hamwe na kūhuna kūria gūcangagīra makīrīa ma gīcunjīkīa mīrongo īnana harī igana kūrī nyamū ciothe dwaru.

English: However, dystocia, vaginal prolapse, endometritis, and pyometra also found to affect the fertility of dairy cattle.
Kikuyu: O na kūrī ūguo, kūremwo na kūgiīka hīndī ya gūciara, vaginal prolapse, endometritis, na pyometra nī īngī ya mīrīmū irīa īrehaga thīīna wa ūciari kūrī ng'ombe cia iria

English: How do I ensure proper hygiene and sanitation in the milking parlor to prevent milk contamination?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingeka atīa gūkinyanīria ūtheru njoku-inī nīguo iria rīkorwo rīrīi theru?

English: Practices included milking parlour cleanliness, hand and udder cleaning, milking and storage containers, cleaning of milk containers, cow's vaccination and treatment and discarding milk from sick or treated cows
Kikuyu: Mītugo mīega nī hamwe na gūtheria njoku, gūthambia moko na nyondo hamwe na indo ciothe cia gūkamīra na kūiga iria, gūthecithia njanjo na kūrigita, na gūte iria rīothe rīa ng'ombe ndwaru kana iria iraheo dawa cia kūrigita.

English: What are the advantages of zero grazing?
Kikuyu: Umithio wa ūrīithi wa kūhingīra ng'ombe nī ūrīkū?

English: Cows are confined and therefore use most of the energy from feeds for growth and milk production.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe ihingagirwo na kuogwo ikahūthīra hinya mūingī wa kuuma irio-inī gūkūra na gūthondeka iria.

English: • Saves land for other enterprises by allowing the use of high yielding fodder crops like napier
Kikuyu: Nīkūhonokagia mūgūnda ūngīhūthīrwo na njīra ingī ta kūhanda mahuti mangīkorwo na ūmithio mūnene ta thara.

English: • Clean milk production is possible.
Kikuyu: Nīkūhotekithagia ūtheru wa iria.

English: • Good calf rearing is possible
Kikuyu: Nīkūhotekithagia mūrerere mwega wa njaū.

English: • Manure can be easily collected for the benefit of fodder crops
Kikuyu: Thumu ni ūcokanagīrīrio kwa njīra hūthū na gūtwarwo mūgūnda wa mahuti.

English: • The animals are better protected against diseases, especially tick-borne diseases.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe nīigitagīrwo kuuma na mīrimū, makīria īrīa īrehagwo nī ngūha.

English: • Close observation of the animals is possible, making heat detection and attendance to animals easier and faster.
Kikuyu: Kūrora ng'ombe wega na kūmenya rīrīa irī mūrukīū o na gūcitungata nī kūhūthaga.

English: • The animals are kept inside which is more secure and protect them from attack by predators • Little or no time is spent on herding the animals. Therefore labour for other farm tasks is saved
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe hingīre nīngitīre kuuma kūrī thū. Ningī nī kūhonokagia ihinda rīa kūrīithia, ūguo ihinda rīu rīkahūthīka kūruta wīra ūngī.

English: What are the disadvantages of zero grazing?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū moru mangīkorūo moimanīte na ūrīithi wa kūhingīra ng'ombe?

English: Much labour is required to take feed and water to the animals.
Kikuyu: Nī wīra mūnene wa kūhe ng'ombe irio na maaī.

English: • Much capital is required for construction of a ‘zero-grazing unit.
Kikuyu: Ningī mbeca nyingī nīibataranagia rīrīa kiūgū gīgwakwo.

English: • The possibility that animals are stressed because of too much confinement inside the zero grazing unit
Kikuyu: Nī kūrī ūgwati wa ng'ombe gūtangīka nīūndū wa kūhingīrwo ihinda iraya.

English: What are the key considerations for construction of a zero grazing unit?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū ma bata maroragwo waki-inī wa ciugū cia ng’ombe?

English: Ensure that the fundi (constructor) is supervised by a Livestock Extension Officer during the construction of the unit.
Kikuyu: Tigīrira fundi niaranyabarwo nī abithaa wa mahiū rīrīa araka nīguo kūrigīrīria mahītia kūhanīka waki-inī.

English: This is necessary because some parts (ie walking area, troughs) once constructed are permanent.
Kikuyu: Ūyū nīūndū wabata nīūndū mwako ūmwe ta waharīa ng'ombe īkinyaga īkīria harīkia gwako nī hagūtūra.

English: Mistakes made during construction can be very costly.
Kikuyu: Mahītia ma mīako nomakorwo goro kūrūnga.

English: ii. Ensure that the correct site, considering the direction of wind, is chosen for the unit.
Kikuyu: ii. Tigīrira gwakwo mūgūnda-inīharīa hagarīire, kūringana na kūrīa rūhuho rūhurutanaga.

English: The choice of site influences the security and protection of animals from rain, sunshine and other weather effects.
Kikuyu: Gūthuurwo kwa handū harīa mahiū megwakīrwo nīgūteithagia harī kūgitīra mahiū mau kuumana na mbura, riūa, na maūndū mangī ma rīera.

English: iii. The unit should be closer to the house and on the opposite side of the wind. The location of the unit in relation to the house should ensure minimal smell from manure pit.
Kikuyu: iii. Kiugū gībatiī gwakwo hakūhī na mūciī mwena ūrīa rūhuho rūtahurutanaga mūno. Mwena ūyū ūnyihie mūhera wa kuuma kiungu-inī ūrīa ūngīkorwo ūgicoka na mūciī.

English: It should be noted that it is more important to protect an animal from the rain than from wind or sunshine.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūmenya atī kūgitīra ng'ombe kuuma kūrī mbura nī gwabata makīria kūrī kuuma kūrī riūa kana rūhuho.

English: iv. Use of local materials for construction of the unit will reduce cost.
Kikuyu: iv. Ūhuthīri wa indo cia gwaka iria ironekwa itūra-inī rīaku nīkūnyihagia ngarama ya waki.

English: v. Finally, one should as much as possible, carry out regular maintenance of the zero-grazing unit while in use. This is usually very important for the walking area.
Kikuyu: v. Mūthia, nīwega kwirūmbūiya na gūthodekaga ciūgu haria ciathūka. Ūyū kaingī nīūndū wabata mūno makīria nīūndū wa harīa ng’ombe ithiagīra kana īrūgamaga īkīria.

English: How do I manage temperature and humidity levels in dairy cattle housing to prevent heat stress?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīhota gūtigīrīra atīa ūrugarī na ūgunyu thīinī wa kiūgū irī īkīro-inī njega nīguo ng’ombe itigatangīke?

English: By providing dairy cows with plenty of drinking water all the time, providing shade and ventilation.
Kikuyu: Ng'ombe irabatara kūheyo maaī kwa wūingī, gwakīrwo kiugū kīna rūgito na kīretekīria rīera gūcera wega.

English: Some farmers with resources can use fans to help cows dissipate heat.
Kikuyu: Arīmi amwe nīmarī ūhoti wa kūgūra ibīrīrīki cia kūhūra ūrugarī ciūgu-inī cia ng'ombe.

English: In warm areas with bright sunlight and when there are no shade trees, the temperatures within the shed can be too high to be comfortable for dairy cattle.
Kikuyu: Ng’ombe nīiheraga rīrīa irī kūndū kūrī na riūa ihiū ona kūrī na ciīruru cia mītī.

English: This effect can be overcome by having an overlay of cadjan or straw or by having a warm air outlet on the roof.  The warm air outlet can be provided by having the roof at two levels
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūyū no ūgirīrīke na njīra ya kwongerera mūgeka kana nyeki rūngū rwa mambarita kana gūthondeka njīra ya kūruta rīera rīrīa ihiū. Njīra īno noithondekwo na gwaka ngito igīrī, īmwe igūrū wa īria īngī.

English: What are the characteristics of a good floor in dairy production?
Kikuyu: Maūthī mega ma floo njega ya kiūgū kīa ng'ombe nī marīkū?
